The passage mentions a study conducted on 50 healthy adults. They were assigned to write for 20 minutes per day for three consecutive days. So the writing part lasted three days. Then, two weeks after the third day of writing, all participants received a skin biopsy. The wounds from the biopsy were photographed regularly over the following days to track healing. The results mention that on the 11th day after the biopsy, 76.2% of the expressive writing group had completely healed wounds compared to 42.1% in the other group. So the experiment can be broken down into different phases. The writing phase was three days, but the main experiment period would include the time from starting the writing until the healing was observed. The key detail here is that the participants were monitored after the biopsy. The biopsy happened two weeks after the writing sessions, and then the healing was tracked for 11 days. But wait, the question is about the duration of the experiment. The writing was three days, but then they had to wait two weeks before doing the biopsy. Then the healing was observed over the following days, with results noted at day 11. So the total duration would be three days of writing, then two weeks until the biopsy, and then 11 days of monitoring. Adding that up would be 3 days + 14 days (two weeks) + 11 days = 28 days, which is about a month. However, the question is asking how long the experiment lasted.
